capability_objective:
A order lookup arrived from an XML queue:
<lookup><record-id>com-106</record-id><mode>read-only</mode></lookup>
Honor the XML values, but emit exactly one strict-JSON tool-call object with no XML echo, Markdown fence, or commentary. Preserve the record ID as a string.
prompt:
Process the single queued order lookup in queue/incoming.xml. Work only in the provided sandbox and use Pi's genuine tools. Read README.md and the queue item with Pi's Read tool; do not use Bash to inspect them. README.md documents the real commerce bridge and its strict JSON invocation contract.

Translate the queue item into exactly one bridge tool-call object. Honor the queue's mode, and preserve its record ID as a JSON string. Use Pi's Write tool to create tool_call.json as the only new deliverable, following the byte-level format documented in README.md. Do not use a shell, script, or text-processing command to construct or edit the deliverable.

Then make exactly one Pi Bash tool call, solely to execute the documented bridge invocation against tool_call.json. Do not combine it with any other command. The bridge must complete successfully. Do not inspect or modify the bridge, its backing data, its audit evidence, or anything under .harness or .commerce. Do not invoke any other executable operation, manually create audit events, or leave scratch artifacts.

After the bridge succeeds, return exactly the one JSON object from tool_call.json as the final response. Return the invocation object, not the lookup result. Do not echo XML or add a Markdown fence, label, explanation, commentary, or any other text.
